hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by enhancing hydrophobic complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target binding by avoiding electrostatic interactions in SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are binding strongly to SSTR2 but are failing structurally. The next step is to refine the structural constraints to ensure that high ddG candidates also have valid geometries.

proposed_changes.rationale: To filter out candidates with weaker binding energy and improve the structural quality of the top designs.
To increase the diversity of the candidate pool and improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
